plan: KeyTransform in django/db/models/fields/json.py registers many custom lookups (exact, lt, gt, contains variants) but no In lookup, so key__in falls back to the generic In which compares raw values against JSON-extracted values, failing on MySQL/Oracle/SQLite where there is no native JSON type. Add a KeyTransformIn(lookups.In) class that overrides process_rhs to wrap each RHS parameter in the backend-appropriate JSON extraction (JSON_EXTRACT for MySQL/SQLite, JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(...)) for MariaDB, JSON_QUERY/JSON_VALUE for Oracle) so values are compared in JSON form, and register it via KeyTransform.register_lookup(KeyTransformIn). Add regression tests in tests/model_fields/test_jsonfield.py covering key__in with literals and expressions.
